Title: Deleting nodes and edges
Summary: :kbd[Backspace] removes selected nodes or edges, with confirmation.
Body:
## The gesture

Click a node or edge to select. Hold :kbd[Shift] and click to add to the selection. Press :kbd[Backspace] (or :kbd[Delete]) to remove.

## Edge deletion

Removing an edge means removing the choice it represents from the source sequence's line. The line itself stays. After delete, the source sequence's choice array shrinks by one.

If the deleted edge was the last one on its line, the line goes back to having a single dashed placeholder handle.

## Node deletion

Removing a sequence is destructive: it deletes the JSON file from disk and (on save) the matching `.tres`. A confirm modal warns first.

For ghost nodes (sequences referenced by some choice but with no actual file backing them), "delete" sweeps across the folder and clears every NextSequenceId that points at the missing target. This is the cleanup path for "I renamed a sequence and its old name is still ghosting around."

## What does not happen

Deleting a sequence does not orphan delete its referencing choices. They go to dangling state (red dashed edge), and the [Integrity tab](/diagnostics/integrity) flags them. This is deliberate: the right move is usually to fix the reference, not to silently strip it.
